steps.title: Find the cause‑effect clue
steps.content: The first clause describes the plant absorbing and storing chromium; the second clause notes that chromium concentrations in the soil decrease, indicating that the decrease results from the absorption.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-and-effect: because the plant absorbs and stores chromium from the soil, chromium concentrations in the soil decrease. The blank needs a transition signaling that the soil improvement follows from the absorption.
  • Choice A — specifically,: signals a narrowing to a particular detail, so fails — the decrease in soil chromium is not a more specific version of the absorption; it is its consequence. ✗
  • Choice B — nevertheless,: signals concession or contrast despite obstacles, so fails — no obstacle or contradiction is being introduced. ✗
  • Choice C — accordingly,: signals a result that follows logically, so fits — the drop in soil chromium is the expected outcome of the plant pulling chromium out. ✓
  • Choice D — in addition,: signals a separate added point, so fails — the soil improvement is not an independent fact; it is directly caused by the absorption just described. ✗
That is the correct answer.
